name: jira-issue-search
title: Jira Issue Search
description: Search and open Jira issues by key or summary. Browse recently viewed issues or query your Jira instance directly.
author: julien_lemonde

ai.instructions:
You help users search and explore Jira issues. When the user asks about issues, generate a JQL query and call the search-jira-issues tool. Always show the `executedJql` field returned by the tool at the top of your response in a code block. This is the actual query sent to Jira with resolved usernames — always use this exact value, never the JQL you composed yourself.

Additional fields: If the user asks to see extra fields (e.g. labels, sprint, fix version, components, epic link), pass them in the `additionalFields` array. The tool resolves field names automatically — use human-readable names as the user said them. Results will include an `additionalFields` object per issue with those values.

JQL rules:
- Always end with an ORDER BY clause.
- Use standard JQL functions: currentUser(), startOfDay(), startOfWeek(), startOfMonth(), endOfDay(), now()
- For child issues of an epic or feature use: issue in childIssuesOf("ISSUE-KEY")
- Do NOT use linkedIssues() — it is not valid JQL. Use issue in childIssuesOf() for parent/child relationships.
- When a user mentions a person by name, use their display name in assignee conditions: assignee = "First Last"
- Issue type mapping (always use the singular form in JQL): stories/story → Story, bugs/bug → Bug, tasks/task → Task, epics/epic → Epic, features/feature → Feature, subtasks/subtask → Sub-task

Examples:
- "my open bugs" → assignee = currentUser() AND issuetype = Bug AND status != Done ORDER BY created DESC
- "high priority stories this week" → issuetype = Story AND priority = High AND created >= startOfWeek() ORDER BY created DESC
- "child stories of PROJ-123" → issue in childIssuesOf("PROJ-123") AND issuetype = Story ORDER BY created DESC
- "child issues of PROJ-123" → issue in childIssuesOf("PROJ-123") ORDER BY created DESC

search-jira-issues.executedJql: assignee = currentUser() AND issuetype = Bug AND status != Done ORDER BY created DESC

evals.input: @jira-issue-search high priority stories updated this week

search-jira-issues.executedJql: issuetype = Story AND priority = High AND updated >= startOfWeek() ORDER BY updated DESC

evals.input: @jira-issue-search show me child issues of PROJ-123

search-jira-issues.executedJql: issue in childIssuesOf("PROJ-123") ORDER BY created DESC

arguments.jql: issue in childIssuesOf("PROJ-123") ORDER BY created DESC

evals.input: @jira-issue-search show me child stories of PROJ-123

search-jira-issues.executedJql: issue in childIssuesOf("PROJ-123") AND issuetype = Story ORDER BY created DESC

arguments.jql: issue in childIssuesOf("PROJ-123") AND issuetype = Story ORDER BY created DESC

tools.name: search-jira-issues
tools.title: Search Jira Issues
tools.description: Execute a JQL query against Jira and return matching issues. Returns each issue's key, summary, type, status, assignee, priority, parent, story points (when available), and URL. Display names in assignee/reporter conditions are automatically resolved to internal usernames.

commands.name: search-issues
commands.title: Search Issues
commands.subtitle: Jira Issue Search
commands.description: Search recently viewed and all Jira issues via API

preferences.name: useSelectionSearch
preferences.title: Selected Text
preferences.label: Pre-fill search bar with selected text
preferences.description: When enabled, any text selected in another app will automatically populate the search bar when opening this command.

commands.name: open-issue
commands.title: Open Issue
commands.subtitle: Jira Issue Search
commands.description: Open an issue via argument, selection, or clipboard

preferences.name: inputSource
preferences.title: Input Source
preferences.description: Where should this specific command look for the ID by default?

preferences.name: jiraUrl
preferences.title: Jira Base URL
preferences.description: The base URL of your Jira instance

preferences.name: jiraToken
preferences.title: Jira API Token
preferences.description: Your Personal Access Token or API Token, You can find it in your Jira Profile as a User token

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
